config.reme: reme-ai 0.4.1.1, own auto_memory ingest + update_index + bm25_search, isolated venv
config.reproduce: python scripts/bench_reme_h2h.py --n 100 --arms blind,rag,reme,tenet --reme-venv scratchpad/reme-venv --workspace-root docs_scratch/reme_ws --budget-cap 10 --out docs_scratch/reme_h2h_n100.jsonl (pre-ingest first via scripts/reme_preingest.py)

provenance.note: reme arm is ReMe's own pipeline end-to-end (auto_memory session notes -> keyword index -> bm25_search) with fixes required to run at all: max_tokens<=32768 for qwen-flash, stream:false, concurrency 8, update_index before retrieval (auto_memory never builds the bm25 index). All arms share the same reader/judge; reader truncation-repair is arm-neutral.

supplementary_reme_agentic.what: ReMe's OWN answering agent end-to-end (agentic_answer job: ReAct, vector_search + bm25_search + python_execute + session-pivot, max_iters 40, LME_EMBEDDING_STORE=default so its vector index is enabled) on the same ingested workspaces, judged by the same qa_judge. Closes the protocol objection that the main harness's single-shot bm25+shared-reader underuses ReMe.

agentic_vs_reme_bm25.verdict: statistically indistinguishable — the 34% was not a reader artifact

supplementary_reme_agentic.rows: docs/reme_agentic_rows.jsonl (trajectories preserved per workspace in mem_session/)
supplementary_reme_agentic.note_retry: 2 initial 900s subprocess timeouts re-run at 1800s; both scored
